articles.title: Sabalenka Advances in Miami Open with Eye on Sunshine Double
articles.summary: Aryna Sabalenka, the world number one tennis player, advanced to the last 16 of the Miami Open by defeating Caty McNally 6-4, 6-2. This victory extends her winning streak to eight matches as she pursues the 'Sunshine Double,' aiming to become only the fifth woman to win both the Indian Wells and Miami tournaments in the same year. Sabalenka previously won Indian Wells this month. In the next round, she will face China's Zheng Qinwen, who recently returned from elbow surgery and beat a top-20 player. The article also covers other matches: Elena Rybakina won against Marta Kostyuk, Jessica Pegula defeated Leylah Fernandez, and Jelena Ostapenko overcame Jasmine Paolini. These results highlight the competitive nature of the Miami Open and the players' progress toward the championship.

articles.title: BTS Comeback Concert Fallout: Hybe Shares Plummet Amid Attendance Shortfall
articles.summary: Hybe, the entertainment company behind K-pop sensation BTS, experienced a significant drop in its share price after a comeback concert in Seoul attracted far fewer attendees than anticipated. The free event at Gwanghwamun Square, marking the first full-group performance since the members began mandatory military service in 2022, drew an estimated 104,000 fans—less than half of the expected 250,000. This led to a 15.5% decline in Hybe's shares on Monday, reversing recent gains ahead of the band's new album Arirang release and 82-date world tour. The concert was live-streamed globally on Netflix, potentially reducing in-person turnout due to strict crowd control and accessibility. Despite the attendance issue, Arirang sold 3.98 million copies on its first day, highlighting BTS's enduring popularity. However, Hybe faces financial pressure as BTS is its primary income source, with operating profits declining during the hiatus. The K-pop industry has grown more competitive, with rivals like Blackpink and emerging threats like the fictional Kpop Demon Hunters movie franchise, which Netflix plans to tour. This situation underscores the high stakes for BTS's return in a changed market.

articles.title: Unraveling the Mystery of Strontium Ruthenate Through Strain Experiments
articles.summary: Scientists investigated strontium ruthenate (Sr2RuO4), an unconventional superconductor that has baffled researchers since its discovery in 1994 due to its unknown electron pairing and symmetry. To probe its properties, a team from Kyoto University conducted a precision experiment applying controlled shear strain—shifting crystal layers sideways—to thin samples at temperatures as low as 30 K. They measured the superconducting transition temperature (Tc) and found it barely changed, with variations smaller than 10 millikelvin per percent strain. This unexpected result challenges leading theories, particularly those suggesting a two-component superconducting state that should respond strongly to strain. Instead, it points to a one-component state or other unconventional forms. The findings help narrow down viable theories but also create a discrepancy with earlier ultrasound studies, posing new questions for future research. The strain-control method developed could aid in studying other complex superconductors like UPt3.

articles.title: Breaking Newton's Law with a Sound-Levitated Time Crystal
articles.summary: Physicists at New York University have developed a novel time crystal that challenges fundamental physics. This system uses acoustic levitation to suspend styrofoam beads in mid-air, creating a visible and hand-held device. Time crystals, first predicted and confirmed about a decade ago, consist of particles that oscillate in regular cycles. In this experiment, the beads interact through scattered sound waves, leading to nonreciprocal forces where larger beads exert a stronger influence on smaller ones than vice versa. This asymmetry breaks Newton's Third Law of Motion, which states that forces occur in equal and opposite pairs. The research, published in Physical Review Letters, highlights the simplicity and accessibility of the setup compared to previous complex experiments. Potential applications include advancements in quantum computing and data storage, while also offering insights into biological processes like circadian rhythms, which involve similar nonreciprocal interactions. The study emphasizes how basic principles of sound and particle dynamics can reveal new scientific frontiers.

articles.title: Korda's Surprise Victory Over World Number One Alcaraz at Miami Open
articles.summary: In a notable upset at the Miami Open, Sebastian Korda defeated world number one Carlos Alcaraz in a three-set match, with scores of 6-3, 5-7, 6-4. Alcaraz, the top seed, started poorly in the first set, allowing Korda to take an early lead. However, Korda nearly lost his advantage in the second set when he served for the match at 5-4 but was broken by Alcaraz, who rallied to win the set. In the decisive third set, Korda maintained composure, breaking Alcaraz's serve at 4-3 after a forehand error, and held his service games to secure victory on his second match point. Post-match, Alcaraz reflected on his performance, stating that despite the loss, he feels his game is improving and he is on the right track. He acknowledged that lower-ranked opponents like Korda, ranked 36th, often play with more freedom and less pressure against him, which can be challenging. Alcaraz also mentioned his recent success, including winning the Australian Open earlier this year, and his first loss of the season to Daniil Medvedev at Indian Wells. Korda, who has previously reached the quarter-finals in Miami, advances to face either 14th seed Karen Khachanov or qualifier Martin Landaluce in the fourth round, marking a significant achievement as the lowest-ranked player to beat Alcaraz since last year.

articles.title: Technology's Impact on Modern Families in The Magic Faraway Tree
articles.summary: This article explores a new film adaptation of Enid Blyton's The Magic Faraway Tree, focusing on its relevance to contemporary issues. The movie follows a family relocating to the countryside, where children discover a magical tree with whimsical worlds. Actors Andrew Garfield and Claire Foy, who play the parents, discuss how the story addresses modern challenges. Garfield expresses concern about social media's influence on youth, describing how tech companies commodify attention, hijacking consciousness during the technological revolution. He emphasizes the need to divest attention and reconnect with eternal elements like nature. Foy extends the issue beyond social media to all ages, criticizing the illusion of control and calling for government legislation to regulate massive tech companies. She supports action over mere conversation, acknowledging parental efforts. The article highlights the film's blend of timeless storytelling with critiques of technology's pervasive effects on family dynamics and personal well-being.
